title: invitations — model traits
description: Trait contract this module owns (HasInvitations, BelongsToInvitation) and consumes from foundation / tenants / framework. HasInvitations is the primary extension point — every module with an invitable resource composes it on the target model.

owned.description: Composed on any target model that can receive invitations (Tenant, Team, Athlete, Federation, TrialSession, ...). Adds a polymorphic `invitations()` MorphMany relation + fluent helpers to send / list / revoke. Requires the composing model to register its `target_type` key with InvitationTargetRegistry in the owner module's service provider boot.

relations.name: invitations
relations.description: All invitations targeting this row, regardless of state.

relations.name: pendingInvitations
relations.description: Non-terminal invitations (state IN pending / delivered / opened / clicked / bounced).

methods.name: invite
methods.description: Create + persist an Invitation for this target. Params: `role_key`, `inviter` (User|ServiceAccount|null=system), `expires_at`, `channel`, `grants`, `message`. Enforces the `unique_pending_invitation` validation rule + per-tenant rate limits. Dispatches `SendInvitationJob` on success.

methods.name: revokeInvitation
methods.description: Transition a pending invitation to `revoked`. Refuses on terminal states.

notAppliedBy: Application (tenants) — applications don't issue invitations; their tenants do
Domain / DomainRecord / Branding — infrastructure resources, no invitees

owned.description: Composed on models CREATED from an invitation. Adds a nullable `invitation_id` FK + BelongsTo relation for provenance / traceability. Primary consumer: the User row that results from `acceptInvitation()` gets its origin invitation stamped for audit ('this user was invited by X on Y date via role Z'). Optional — most modules don't need it; useful for user, teams, guardians.
appliedBy: User (user) — traceability from invitation to created user row

consumed.description: Variant of BelongsToTenant that allows tenant_id to be NULL. Global scope is applied only when a tenant context is present in the request (i.e. filters out NULL tenant_id on tenant hosts but leaves everything visible on central + platform-admin hosts). Owned by tenants; this module is the first consumer. Needs to be added to tenants/traits.json when authored.
Free-form metadata JSONB column + fluent accessors.

Powers the Invitation state machine. State classes: PendingState, DeliveredState, OpenedState, ClickedState, AcceptedState, DeclinedState, ExpiredState, RevokedState, BouncedState. Transitions registered in `InvitationState::config()`.
created_by / updated_by / deleted_by. NOT applied to InvitationEvent (immutable log).

Audit-trail integration. Invitation logs every state transition here in addition to writing an InvitationEvent row (double-write is deliberate — audits live per-tenant retention, events live per-analytics retention).
Only on Invitation. InvitationEvent is append-only; deletion happens via retention purge only.

compositionExample.note: A target model composing HasInvitations. Tenant is the canonical example.

blueprintExample.note: Migrations compose the paired `->invitable()` macro. It's register-only — no columns are added to the target's table (polymorphism lives on the invitations table itself).
